message:
fcp-core/revocation: style refactor of add_revocation "keep earliest" logic

Refactor the earliest-effective-at preservation added in the
replay-to-defer defense commit, without changing behavior:

* Collapse the explicit `match` into a single `is_none_or` check:
  `should_replace = self.revocations.get(object_id)
    .is_none_or(|existing| existing.effective_at > revocation.effective_at);`
  then `if should_replace { self.revocations.insert(...) }`.
* Fix the rustdoc on `add_revocation` to use code-styled
  `\`object_id\`` instead of the bare `object_id` identifier, so
  `cargo doc` and clippy's doc lint both stop complaining.

Semantics are identical to the previous `match` form:
* No existing entry → insert.
* Existing entry with `effective_at <= new.effective_at` → keep it.
* Existing entry with strictly later effective_at → replace with the
  earlier one.

The `is_none_or` helper reads more naturally for this "default to
replacing unless the existing entry is stricter" pattern and
shortens the code from five match branches (including a comment
branch) to three lines.

No test changes needed; the two existing tests
(`registry_later_revocation_does_not_defer_active_one` and
`registry_earlier_revocation_replaces_later_one`) cover both
branches and still pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
